claims.name: Eq.32 look-elsewhere uniqueness
claims.claim: n=12 is the unique hit (not fitted from a range)
claims.computed: 1 hit(s) within 1% of RHS out of 120 combinations: [('4/3', 12, 'tau/e')]
claims.note: grid = 5 prefactors x 8 exponents x 3 mass pairs

claims.name: N_opt = DM:OM ratio non-integer
claims.claim: N_opt ~ 5.366, deviation from 5 is 5.67 sigma (delta-method), NOT 259 sigma
claims.note: Planck omega_cdm/omega_b. The ~259 sigma is a UNIT-MISMATCH artifact (dimensionless DeltaN over omega-space sigma). Done right, BOTH methods agree: delta-method N-space = 5.67 sigma; correct omega-space (omega_cdm-5 omega_b)/s_tot = 5.79 sigma.

claims.name: Boson squared-mass ratio 7:9:17
claims.claim: 7:9:17 holds at ~0.2% by value, but per-boson sigma varies (W ~3.8 sigma, NOT 0.4)
claims.sigma: worst (W) = 3.81 sigma; Z is the anchor (0 by construction)
claims.note: '0.4 sigma' was H only (best boson); W deviates ~3.8 sigma. Report all three.

claims.name: 7:9:17 look-elsewhere uniqueness (integer pairs n<=50)
claims.claim: r_W=6.9912 [claim:7], r_H=16.9660 [claim:17]; pairs within 2%: [(7, 17)]; MC look-elsewhere at 0.2% in ±5% window: P(random)=1/624
claims.computed: only pair at any threshold up to 2%: (7,17). No alternatives up to n=50.
claims.sigma: look-elsewhere factor ~624; P(random masses hit any integer pair at 0.2%) = 1/624
claims.note: Strengthens C6: (7,17) is unique at even 2% threshold; random EW masses hit integer pair 1 in ~600 times.

claims.name: Delta N_eff for IDM dark sectors
claims.claim: ANY single number is misleading: not computable from preprint (BLOCKED). Naive SM thermal history gives 22-81 (130-477 sigma); '0.70' is one assumed-dilution scenario.
claims.target: depends on T_dark, g_D, T_dark/T_nu -- ALL absent in preprint (6/7 inputs missing)
claims.note: m7_c audit = BLOCKED; mirror_dm = 22-81; '0.70' needs assumed g_*S>210. Do not table one value.
